title: Implement Atomic Transactions with Rollback Capability
description: The system shall implement atomic transactions that ensure all-or-nothing execution with rollback capability on failure.
rationale: To maintain data consistency and prevent partial state changes in the event of operation failures, ensuring reliability and integrity of the system's operations.
business_value: Enhances the robustness and reliability of the system, reducing potential for data corruption and improving user trust by providing consistent outcomes despite errors.

source.description: As a developer I need to implement atomic transactions that ensure all-or-nothing execution with rollback capability on failure So that data remains consistent even if an operation fails

acceptance_criteria:
Given a transaction with multiple operations
When all operations succeed
Then the transaction is committed
And all changes are persisted

Given a transaction with multiple operations
When one or more operations fail
Then the transaction is rolled back
And no changes from the transaction are persisted
preconditions: The system must be operational and accessible.
All necessary resources (e.g., network, storage) must be available.
Valid data must be provided for each operation in the transaction.
postconditions: Data changes made as part of a successful transaction must be persisted and visible to other operations or systems.
Any changes made during a failed transaction must be rolled back, leaving no partial state changes.
functional_constraints: All operations within a transaction must complete atomically; either all succeed and are committed, or all fail and are rolled back.
The system must handle errors gracefully and ensure data consistency even in the presence of system crashes, network failures, or invalid data.
Transactions must not allow partial state changes due to failures.
security_requirements: Data within transactions must be protected from unauthorized access during both execution and rollback phases.

non_functional_requirements.performance: Transaction processing time should be optimized for speed and efficiency.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be highly available with minimal downtime, ensuring data consistency is not compromised due to unavailability.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The transaction handling mechanism must scale to support a growing number of transactions and operations without degradation in performance.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error handling during transaction processing should provide clear feedback to users or systems initiating the transaction.

edge_cases: Invalid data input causing transaction failure
dependencies: Valid and consistent data for operations

interfaces.name: Transaction Management API
interfaces.description: Interface for initiating, committing, rolling back, and querying transactions.

interfaces.name: Data Storage
interfaces.description: Stores data changes made during transactions. Must support atomic operations.

test_cases.description: Verify transaction commits when all operations succeed.
test_cases.expected_result: Transaction is committed, and all changes are persisted in the database.

test_cases.description: Verify transaction rolls back when any operation fails.
test_cases.expected_result: Transaction is rolled back, no changes are persisted in the database.

test_cases.description: Test transaction handling during system crash mid-execution.
test_cases.expected_result: System recovers and rolls back any in-progress transactions.
